changes_made: Removed k8s_service_mesh_mtls_enabled as it does not fit the standard resource categories and is more specific to service mesh implementations rather than core Kubernetes.
review_notes: The control AC-4(12) is applicable to Kubernetes as it involves enforcing secure information flow, which can be achieved through TLS in various components. The function names mostly follow the naming conventions, but 'k8s_service_mesh_mtls_enabled' is not aligned with the standard Kubernetes resources and is more specific to service mesh implementations, which are not part of the core Kubernetes resources. The other functions are well-named and map to real Kubernetes security controls.
technical_concerns: k8s_service_mesh_mtls_enabled is not a standard Kubernetes resource and should be considered in the context of specific service mesh implementations.
naming_improvements: Ensure all function names align with the standard Kubernetes resource categories.
title: Information Flow Enforcement - Information flow enforcement | data type identifiers when transferring informati
step1_reasoning: The control AC-4(12) focuses on enforcing secure information flow, particularly through the use of data type identifiers and secure transport mechanisms like TLS. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring secure communication between components and enforcing TLS for ingress and service mesh communications. NetworkPolicies can be used to enforce secure communication paths, while Ingress configurations and service mesh settings can enforce TLS. Additionally, ensuring that the API server uses TLS for secure communication aligns with this control.
